summary:
Elderly people consuming both tobacco and alcohol have high risks but aren’t exclusively at fault; other factors like HPVs also contribute importantly. Age matters contextually rather strictly by years lived post-risk onset or cumulative exposures matter most over time irrespective specific ages given similar habits persistently present together. To summarize:

- High risk includes those aged >40 combined heavy users of tobacco & alcohol along with additional modifiable/controllable hazards including viral etiologies.
- Comprehensive assessment should account for multiple contributing elements beyond simple demographic markers. Summary: Individuals older than 40 who use both tobacco and alcohol heavily face heightened risk, though various other factors such as HPV infection can also increase susceptibility. Assessments must consider multiple contributors extending beyond basic demographic indicators.
